summary: VibeRidez Cargo Master + License Inbox final pre-beta verification. Backend regression_shield 614/614 PASS. New cargo e2e suite (15 tests) + service unit settlement test (1 test) all PASS — full pickup→handover→verify→80/20 settlement with burn=0 invariant proven. Frontend /driver/cargo and /artist/dashboard render all required data-testids; LicenseInboxCard live-polls real royalty rows for the seeded artist.

issues: Cmd+K / Ctrl+K keyboard shortcut did not open the Explore search palette on /dashboard from a headless browser. The 'Cargo Driver Console' entry IS present in EXPLORE_REGISTRY (Explore.tsx:67), so search works once the palette is opened via the UI button — main agent should verify whether a global hotkey handler is mounted on /dashboard.

action_items: (Optional) Confirm Cmd+K global hotkey is mounted on /dashboard route — palette did not open from keyboard in headless test; this may be UA-specific (e.g., listener attached to a specific component).
critical_code_review_comments: viberidez_cargo.py:_settle_cargo absorbs 80/20 rounding into platform_share (cargo_value - store_share). Good — never under-pays the store, dust goes to recirculation, no burn. Confirmed burn_coins=0 hard-coded.

place_retail_order debits coins BEFORE decrementing inventory; if the inventory $inc somehow fails after debit, the customer is charged for nothing. Recommend wrapping both in a Mongo transaction or rolling back the debit on decrement failure (low risk since both ops touch independent collections and Mongo writes are durable, but worth a follow-up).

driver_scan_pickup allows manifest.driver_id == None (line 262) — meaning ANY authenticated user could call /api/cargo/pickup on an UNASSIGNED manifest if they happened to know its id and the pickup plaintext. Since the plaintext is returned in the order-place response to the customer, the customer could self-pickup before a driver is assigned. Recommend tightening: require driver_id to be non-null AND admin-assigned before pickup is allowed. (Same applies to the placing customer knowing pickup_pt → they shouldn't be able to act as driver.) HIGH-PRIORITY pre-beta.

place_retail_order returns pickup_barcode_plaintext to the CUSTOMER in the order response (line 235 of service). This leaks the driver's pickup secret to the customer — combined with the above driver_id=None hole, this is a delivery-bypass risk. Recommend removing pickup_barcode_plaintext from the customer order-place response; only the assigned driver should see it via /api/cargo/driver/manifest/{id}.
Driver console endpoint /api/cargo/driver/me/assignments correctly strips both hashes and plaintexts ✅; get_manifest correctly reveals only the active-state plaintext ✅.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested testids (driver-cargo-page, assignments-section, active-manifest-section, driver-cargo-refresh, assignments-empty, active-empty, license-inbox-card, inbox-refresh-btn, inbox-manage-splits-link all present)

test_credentials: betatester1@globalvibez.com / BetaTester2026! (used). betatester2@globalvibez.com used as customer in e2e order placement.
seed_data_creation: Test inventory SKUs prefixed TEST_; unit test creates TEST_owner/TEST_cust/TEST_drv user ids and cleans up after.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend cargo flow fully covered. Two security findings in code review are HIGH priority for main agent: (1) pickup endpoint allows driver_id=None manifests + (2) customer receives pickup plaintext on order placement. Together these let the customer self-complete pickup. License Inbox is rendering 2 real fan_tip royalty rows for betatester1 — successful integration.
